avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be a rapid increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes, and at the base of rock walls. The runout zones are to be treated with caution. Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in some cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also on steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.
Caution is to be exercised in particular in little used terrain, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.
The no longer entirely fresh wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The old snowpack will be prone to triggering in some places. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. This applies in particular on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is unfavourably layered and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

tendency.highlights:
Steep shady slopes high altitudes: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. Steep sunny slopes intermediate and high altitudes: The danger of wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m and in all aspects at elevated altitudes. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. Whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

As the moisture increases small to medium-sized wet avalanches are to be expected. In the event of an overcast night this applies in particular. Caution is to be exercised in particular below approximately 2200 m. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
On Sunday it will be mild over a wide area. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times.

Shady slopes: The snowpack remains prone to triggering in some places. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already soften in the late morning. The weather conditions gave rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Wet snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also on steep sunny slopes above approximately 2400 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted over a wide area. Individual small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized wet loose snow avalanches are possible below approximately 2200 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall in some localities. The weather will be mild.

Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches as before.

The weather will be sunny over a wide area. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in isolated cases in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances. Until the temperature falls moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. The weather will be very mild. These weather conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack.
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Some snow will fall in some regions.

bulletins.highlights: The weather was very mild. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase significantly during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent barely recognisable for beginners.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment: The dangerous spots are found on steep slopes, especially grassy ones, as well as in areas near the ridges and at the base of rock walls, also due to the possibility of debris falling from above. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on sunny slopes. Gliding avalanches are also to be expected. The wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering. Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment: The weather conditions will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In particular very steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Early morning: In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
In particular very steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized wet and gliding avalanches are to be expected below approximately 2300 m.

Weak layers exist deeper in the snowpack. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places. These can be triggered in deep layers and reach large size. These avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 2300 m.
Sunday: In some regions some new snow above approximately 1500 m. The weather conditions fostered a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations in particular on sunny slopes.
In its middle, the snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. There are large frames on the crest.
Numerous medium-sized to large moist and wet avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: As the temperature drops only isolated moist and wet avalanches are to be expected. Light precipitation in the south-eastern sector, snow at 1200-1500 m. New snow is lying on a hard crust.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: As the day progresses as a consequence of the rain there will be only a slight increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.
avalancheActivity.comment:
At low and intermediate altitudes and at low and intermediate altitudes individual moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads.

There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. This applies especially in particular at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions fostered a gradual strengthening of the snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The rain will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The wind slabs can be released easily in some places in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather conditions fostered a substantial stabilisation of the snowpack.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

In particular on shady slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach medium size in some cases. They can as before be released, in particular by large loads. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. In addition, only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.

Some snow will fall in some localities. The rain will give rise as the day progresses to slight moistening of the snowpack in some cases in particular at low altitude.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases. The danger of wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.

As a consequence of warming during the day individual wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to gradual softening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. Avalanches can in very isolated cases be triggered in the old snowpack and reach medium size. In all aspects small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized wet avalanches are possible until the temperature drops.

snowpackStructure.comment: Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack over a wide area.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Some snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slight increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of the precipitation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the precipitation more moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses. This applies in particular on grassy slopes, as well as at low and intermediate altitudes.
Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.

There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. This applies in particular on steep slopes in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and.
The rain will give rise as the day progresses to slight moistening of the snowpack.
